rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The metrics-collector ServiceAccount in demo-rbac lacks the RBAC permission to list pods in its own namespace, causing the readiness probe to fail and the Deployment to be unavailable.
causalChain: Signal: RBACPolicyDenied — Deployment `metrics-collector` in `demo-rbac` is flagged for RBAC policy denial
Why? The pod's readiness probe (`kubectl auth can-i list pods -n demo-rbac`) returns 'no', causing the pod to be marked NotReady and the Deployment unavailable
Why? The `metrics-collector` ServiceAccount does not have a valid RoleBinding granting it `list` on `pods` in the `demo-rbac` namespace — confirmed by pod logs showing FORBIDDEN from 17:03:43 UTC

Why? The required Role and RoleBinding for the `metrics-collector` ServiceAccount are absent or were deleted — regression history shows 3 prior remediation attempts with the same spec hash, indicating the binding is repeatedly removed

Root cause: Missing or repeatedly-deleted RoleBinding (and/or Role) in namespace `demo-rbac` that would grant the `metrics-collector` ServiceAccount permission to `list pods` — the RBAC resources needed for this workload are not durably present in the cluster

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: The remediation target is `Deployment/metrics-collector` because the RBAC workflow needs to create a Role + RoleBinding for this workload's ServiceAccount. The Deployment is the root owner of the affected pod and ServiceAccount. A workflow targeting this Deployment can create the necessary RBAC resources in the `demo-rbac` namespace.
dueDiligence.proportionality: The problem is scoped to a single Deployment in one namespace. The fix is targeted: create a Role + RoleBinding for the `metrics-collector` ServiceAccount in `demo-rbac`. No cluster-wide changes needed.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: Deployment, ReplicaSet, Pod (describe, logs, events), ServiceAccount, Namespace, Prometheus RBAC metrics, resource context with remediation history. Not investigated: ClusterRoleBindings for this SA (agent lacked permission), audit logs showing who deleted the RoleBinding. Upstream/downstream: no services depend on this Deployment (it is a monitoring/collector workload).
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from signal → readiness probe failure → RBAC denial ('no'/'FORBIDDEN') → missing RoleBinding → repeated regression. Cannot go deeper without cluster audit logs showing who/what is deleting the RoleBinding. The regression history (3 prior attempts) strongly suggests a controller or process is removing the binding.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: Claims backed by: pod logs (FORBIDDEN from 17:03:43), pod events (readiness probe returning 'no' at 17:03:44, 5 occurrences), pod condition (ready: false, ContainersNotReady), Deployment status (MinimumReplicasUnavailable), resource context (regression_detected: true, 3 prior remediations). Unverified: the exact Role/RoleBinding names (investigation agent lacked permission to list RBAC resources). The absence of a binding is inferred from the denial behavior, not directly confirmed by listing RoleBindings.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: Remediation history shows 3 prior tier-1 remediations (at 16:32:47, 16:42:43, 16:57:24 UTC) all with the same pre-remediation spec hash, and `regression_detected: true`. Previous remediations apparently applied a fix that was subsequently undone. The new remediation approach should investigate why the RoleBinding is being deleted (e.g., a GitOps controller reverting it) and ensure the binding is added to the authoritative source of truth, not just applied ad-hoc.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: 1) Network policy blocking kubectl API calls — ruled out: the pod successfully called the API at startup and no network events were observed. 2) ServiceAccount token issue — ruled out: the pod authenticated successfully for the first ~40 seconds. 3) API server issue — ruled out: Prometheus shows only 'allowed' decisions and the API is serving other requests. 4) Image or application bug — ruled out: the probe command is `kubectl auth can-i`, a standard CLI operation that directly reflects RBAC state.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.88: Starting at 1.0. -0.05 for inability to directly list/describe RoleBindings (agent permission gap — absence of binding inferred, not directly confirmed). -0.05 for not knowing who/what is deleting the RoleBinding (audit logs inaccessible). -0.02 for the regression pattern suggesting a deeper systemic issue (GitOps drift?) that may require more than just re-creating the binding.

contributingFactors: The readiness probe uses `kubectl auth can-i list pods -n demo-rbac` — directly dependent on RBAC permissions for the ServiceAccount.
The container's main loop also calls `kubectl get pods -n demo-rbac`, so the RBAC denial affects both liveness and core application function.

Regression detected: 3 prior remediation attempts with the same pre-remediation spec hash (sha256:c5720cf16ef66b41604a9705c3445aa6a16ed77ca42292b953d4c2ecdeacd9cf), indicating the Role/RoleBinding granting `list pods` is being repeatedly removed or never durably applied.

The ServiceAccount `metrics-collector` exists in `demo-rbac` but no RoleBinding granting it `list pods` on the Pods resource is currently active — confirmed by the probe returning 'no' and pod logs showing FORBIDDEN from ~17:03:43 UTC.
Investigation agent could not directly list RoleBindings/Roles in demo-rbac (forbidden), so the absence of a binding is inferred from the denial behavior.

kaResponse.analysis: The metrics-collector ServiceAccount in demo-rbac lacks the RBAC permission to list pods in its own namespace, causing the readiness probe to fail and the Deployment to be unavailable. Pod logs confirm 'FORBIDDEN' errors from 17:03:43 UTC onward, and the readiness probe (kubectl auth can-i list pods -n demo-rbac) began returning 'no' at 17:03:44 UTC. A RoleBinding granting the metrics-collector ServiceAccount list/get/watch on pods in demo-rbac is absent or has been repeatedly removed, as evidenced by 3 prior remediation attempts all matching the same pre-remediation spec hash — indicating a regression pattern.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The metrics-collector ServiceAccount in demo-rbac lacks the RBAC permission to list pods in its own namespace, causing the readiness probe to fail and the Deployment to be unavailable. Pod logs confirm 'FORBIDDEN' errors from 17:03:43 UTC onward, and the readiness probe (kubectl auth can-i list pods -n demo-rbac) began returning 'no' at 17:03:44 UTC. A RoleBinding granting the metrics-collector ServiceAccount list/get/watch on pods in demo-rbac is absent or has been repeatedly removed, as evidenced by 3 prior remediation attempts all matching the same pre-remediation spec hash — indicating a regression pattern.
causalChain: Signal: RBACPolicyDenied — Deployment `metrics-collector` in `demo-rbac` is flagged for RBAC policy denial
Why? The pod's readiness probe (`kubectl auth can-i list pods -n demo-rbac`) returns 'no', causing the pod to be marked NotReady and the Deployment unavailable
Why? The `metrics-collector` ServiceAccount does not have a valid RoleBinding granting it `list` on `pods` in the `demo-rbac` namespace — confirmed by pod logs showing FORBIDDEN from 17:03:43 UTC

Why? The required Role and RoleBinding for the `metrics-collector` ServiceAccount are absent or were deleted — regression history shows 3 prior remediation attempts with the same spec hash, indicating the binding is repeatedly removed

Root cause: Missing or repeatedly-deleted RoleBinding (and/or Role) in namespace `demo-rbac` that would grant the `metrics-collector` ServiceAccount permission to `list pods` — the RBAC resources needed for this workload are not durably present in the cluster

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: The remediation target is `Deployment/metrics-collector` because the RBAC workflow needs to create a Role + RoleBinding for this workload's ServiceAccount. The Deployment is the root owner of the affected pod and ServiceAccount. A workflow targeting this Deployment can create the necessary RBAC resources in the `demo-rbac` namespace.
dueDiligence.proportionality: The problem is scoped to a single Deployment in one namespace. The fix is targeted: create a Role + RoleBinding for the `metrics-collector` ServiceAccount in `demo-rbac`. No cluster-wide changes needed.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: Deployment, ReplicaSet, Pod (describe, logs, events), ServiceAccount, Namespace, Prometheus RBAC metrics, resource context with remediation history. Not investigated: ClusterRoleBindings for this SA (agent lacked permission), audit logs showing who deleted the RoleBinding. Upstream/downstream: no services depend on this Deployment (it is a monitoring/collector workload).
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from signal → readiness probe failure → RBAC denial ('no'/'FORBIDDEN') → missing RoleBinding → repeated regression. Cannot go deeper without cluster audit logs showing who/what is deleting the RoleBinding. The regression history (3 prior attempts) strongly suggests a controller or process is removing the binding.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: Claims backed by: pod logs (FORBIDDEN from 17:03:43), pod events (readiness probe returning 'no' at 17:03:44, 5 occurrences), pod condition (ready: false, ContainersNotReady), Deployment status (MinimumReplicasUnavailable), resource context (regression_detected: true, 3 prior remediations). Unverified: the exact Role/RoleBinding names (investigation agent lacked permission to list RBAC resources). The absence of a binding is inferred from the denial behavior, not directly confirmed by listing RoleBindings.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: Remediation history shows 3 prior tier-1 remediations (at 16:32:47, 16:42:43, 16:57:24 UTC) all with the same pre-remediation spec hash, and `regression_detected: true`. Previous remediations apparently applied a fix that was subsequently undone. The new remediation approach should investigate why the RoleBinding is being deleted (e.g., a GitOps controller reverting it) and ensure the binding is added to the authoritative source of truth, not just applied ad-hoc.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: 1) Network policy blocking kubectl API calls — ruled out: the pod successfully called the API at startup and no network events were observed. 2) ServiceAccount token issue — ruled out: the pod authenticated successfully for the first ~40 seconds. 3) API server issue — ruled out: Prometheus shows only 'allowed' decisions and the API is serving other requests. 4) Image or application bug — ruled out: the probe command is `kubectl auth can-i`, a standard CLI operation that directly reflects RBAC state.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: 0.88: Starting at 1.0. -0.05 for inability to directly list/describe RoleBindings (agent permission gap — absence of binding inferred, not directly confirmed). -0.05 for not knowing who/what is deleting the RoleBinding (audit logs inaccessible). -0.02 for the regression pattern suggesting a deeper systemic issue (GitOps drift?) that may require more than just re-creating the binding.

contributingFactors: The readiness probe uses `kubectl auth can-i list pods -n demo-rbac` — directly dependent on RBAC permissions for the ServiceAccount.
The container's main loop also calls `kubectl get pods -n demo-rbac`, so the RBAC denial affects both liveness and core application function.
Regression detected: 3 prior remediation attempts with the same pre-remediation spec hash, indicating the Role/RoleBinding granting `list pods` is being repeatedly removed or never durably applied.

The ServiceAccount `metrics-collector` exists in `demo-rbac` but no RoleBinding granting it `list pods` on the Pods resource is currently active — confirmed by the probe returning 'no' and pod logs showing FORBIDDEN from ~17:03:43 UTC.
